data.body: PASS-1 review (G6 — approach soundness): PASS, no findings. Mechanism verified against the implemented surface (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py, orchestrator.material_fingerprint, src/rebar/signing.py, docs/reuse-surface.md §1). The chosen approach — reuse the close-gate HMAC-SHA256 signing, bind the material fingerprint INTO the signed manifest (so HMAC protects it), invalidate by recompute-and-compare plus inherited code-HEAD freshness — is logic-complete, has no TOCTOU (the fingerprint is inside the signed payload), fails closed on an unrecoverable fingerprint, and converges LWW on concurrent signs. Material scope (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition; not tags/comments/links/assignee) matches material_fingerprint() exactly. Reviewer-generated alternatives (separate key custody; storing the fingerprint as an unsigned sibling field) are both strictly worse on blast-radius/robustness; the chosen approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on every axis. Positive rationale is stated (Why: composable CI rigor / anti-vibe-coding signal; Scope: no new key custody, integrity/forgery/replay/staleness model inherited).
